class.name: ResonanceMaterial
class.brief_description: Acoustic material for Steam Audio. Controls absorption, scattering, and transmission per frequency band.
class.description: Used by [ResonanceGeometry] to define how surfaces reflect, absorb, and transmit sound. Presets in addons/nexus_resonance/materials/ are aligned with Steam Audio/Valve reference presets from phonon.h. Standard variants (approx. 30 cm): concrete, glass, wood, metal, fabric, carpet, marble_tile, brick, drywall, plaster, rubber, water, acoustic_ceiling_tile, gravel_sand, plastic, ceramic. Thin variants (5-10 cm): same names with "_thin" suffix, higher transmission.

members.member: High frequency absorption (8 kHz and above). 0 = none, 1 = full absorption.
Low frequency absorption (up to 800 Hz). 0 = none, 1 = full absorption.
Mid frequency absorption (800 Hz to 8 kHz). 0 = none, 1 = full absorption.
Surface roughness for reflections. 0 = mirror-like, 1 = diffuse.
Low frequency transmission (up to 800 Hz). How much sound passes through. 0 = blocked, 1 = full.
Mid frequency transmission (800 Hz to 8 kHz). How much sound passes through. 0 = blocked, 1 = full.
High frequency transmission (8 kHz and above). How much sound passes through. 0 = blocked, 1 = full.
Convenience setter: sets low/mid/high transmission to the same value. For per-band control use transmission_low/mid/high.
